title: Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple
tagline: Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple is a Hindu Temple located in Thanikkudam of Thrissur city in Kerala, on the banks of Puzhakkal River, also called Thanikkudam river.
overview.description: Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple is a Hindu Temple located in Thanikkudam of Thrissur city in Kerala, on the banks of Puzhakkal River, also called Thanikkudam river. It is dedicated to Goddess Bhadrakali, in the form of 'Nanadurga'. It is famous for 'arattu' ceremony during monsoon season, when the river is flooded and enters the main sanctum, thus submerging the whole idol. This temples is one of the rare temples with such a ceremony.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thanikkudam
topPlaces.description: Thanikkudam is a rapidly growing suburb north-east of Thrissur, in the Indian state of Kerala. The village-town is home to the Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple which is popularly known across the central part of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kurichikkara
topPlaces.description: Kurichikkara is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Madakkathara
topPlaces.description: Madakkathara is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Cashew Research Station, Madakkathara
topPlaces.description: Cashew Research Station, Madakkathara is a research Station under the Central Zone of Kerala Agricultural University at Madakkathara in Thrissur district of Kerala, India. This research Station was established in 1973.

topPlaces.name: Poomala Dam
topPlaces.description: Poomala Dam is an irrigation purpose dam and a tourist spot situated in Mulankunnathukavu Panchayath under Puzhakkal block in Thrissur, Kerala State of India.

topPlaces.name: Kuttumuck Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Kuttumuck Siva Temple, the abode of Lord Kuttumuck Sree Mahadevan, is located about 5 km northeast of Thrissur. According to the legends, the idol worshipped here is more than 300 years old.

topPlaces.name: Ramavarmapuram
topPlaces.description: Ramavarmapuram is the northern suburb of Thrissur City in Kerala. It is home to several governmental and other institutions.

topPlaces.name: Kuttumukku
topPlaces.description: Kuttumukku is a ward of Thrissur Municipal Corporation situated in the north-east side of the Corporation. It is a residential area with only a few institutions of importance.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (49 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (67 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thanikkudam Bhagavathi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thanikkudam, Kurichikkara, Madakkathara, Cashew Research Station, Madakkathara, Poomala Dam.
